key_insight: Midpoint-fused arches can snap between two stable, force-free shapes, and the paper explains this bistability with an energy-based pinned-pinned model.
engineering_relevance: This provides a design framework for deployable or reconfigurable shell/arch structures that must toggle reliably between configurations.
method: Minimize total potential energy for pinned-pinned midpoint-fused arches under a concentrated mid-span load
Derive kinematic compatibility at the fusion point to couple multiple arches
Use symmetry assumptions to reduce the nonlinear deformation problem and analyze 2-arch and 3-arch cases
evidence_signal: The analytical predictions are validated against nonlinear finite-element simulations and table-top experiments, with close agreement in both equilibrium states and transition responses.
watch_item: The model depends on symmetry choices and ideal pinned-pinned boundary conditions, so real structures with manufacturing imperfections or boundary compliance may deviate.
paper_title: Bistability of midpoint-fused arches with pinned-pinned boundary conditions
